case_title: ANTHONY E. HELD VS. THE KITCHEN COLLECTION LLC et al

MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF HELD, ANTHONY E. PHD PE

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANTHONY HELD'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROP 65 SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH HEALTH AND SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7. WHILE THE COURT HAS SOME CONCERNS ABOUT THE REASONABLENESS OF THE FEES AGREED TO BY THE PARTIES AND THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE EVIDENTIARY SHOWING MADE BY PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL OF THE FEES, UNLESS AND UNTIL THE COURT HAS DEVELOPED A BETTER SYSTEM OF EVALUATING THE REASONABLENESS OF FEES AGREED TO BY THE PARTIES IN PROPOSITION 65 CASES WHERE THERE IS NO OPPOSITION MADE TO THOSE FEES, THE COURT IS SATISFIED THAT THE SHOWING MADE HERE IS ADEQUATE, ALTHOUGH PERHAPS BARELY SO. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-07-2012 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement;
Off Calendar Jun-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Josh Vorghees Esq., of The Chanler Group, appearing for plaintiff and moving party.
Rebecca L. Woodson, Esq., of McKenna Long & Aldridge, Tel. 415.267.4137, appearing for defendant.
Robert S. Schuda, Esq., of McKenna Long & Aldridge, Tel. 213.243.6136, appearing for defendant via court call.

The tentative ruling was not opposed. The court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ANTHONY HELD'S Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement is Granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with Health and Safety Code section 25249.7. While the court has some concerns about the reasonableness of the fees agreed to by the parties and the sufficiency of the evidentiary showing made by plaintiff for approval of the fees, unless and until the court has developed a better system of evaluating the reasonableness of fees agreed to by the parties in Proposition 65 cases where there is no opposition made to those fees, the court is satisfied that the showing made here is adequate, although perhaps barely so. Order and Judgment signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-21-2012 Set For Trial On Sep-24-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
